Spotted a Forest Emergency?
If you witness a wildfire, illegal logging, poaching, or an injured animal — report it now. Every second counts for Sri Lanka's forests and wildlife.
🔥 Report Fire 🐘 Wildlife Incident
❓ Support

Frequently Asked Questions

You can report illegal deforestation through our emergency hotline (1990), the RootNet mobile app's "Report" feature, or by emailing info@rootnet.lk with photos and the location. Rangers will be dispatched immediately for urgent cases.

Yes! Verified academic researchers, NGOs, and government agencies can apply for data access. Email research@rootnet.lk with your institution details and intended use. Public datasets are also available on our open data portal.

We welcome partnerships with conservation NGOs, technology companies, universities, and international environmental bodies. Please reach out via the contact form above selecting "Partnership & Collaboration", or email info@rootnet.lk with a brief proposal.

Volunteer ranger registrations are handled quarterly. Sign up through the RootNet community portal, attend a mandatory one-day orientation, and you'll be assigned to a forest zone near you. Training is provided free of charge.

The core platform — including forest maps, species tracking, and alert feeds — is completely free for the public. Advanced API access and real-time data streaming for institutional users may require a verified account and approval.

Join the RootNet Community

Whether you're a researcher, ranger, student, or nature lover — RootNet is for everyone who cares about Sri Lanka's forests. Sign up free and start exploring today.
